edges.description: Selective substrate hypoacetylation perturbs fibroblast cell-cycle and motility programs
Cell-cycle and migration defects provide a plausible bridge from selective NatA substrate hypoacetylation to the severe neurodevelopmental phenotype.
Developmental and tissue-maintenance defects converge on the recognizable aged appearance, craniofacial gestalt, and growth failure.

edges.description: Ogden syndrome includes structural and electrophysiologic cardiac involvement downstream of the NAA10/NatA defect.
The postnatal clinical course includes neonatal intensive-care needs for respiratory distress and feeding problems.
The recurrent classic phenotype includes cryptorchidism, consistent with multisystem developmental involvement.
Neurodevelopmental involvement manifests as severe global developmental delay.
Broader NAA10-related Ogden cohorts include intellectual disability.
Neuromotor involvement manifests as hypotonia.
Neurologic involvement can manifest as seizures.
Structural brain involvement includes cerebral atrophy on imaging.
The progeroid tissue phenotype is expressed clinically as prematurely aged appearance.

edges.description: The recognizable craniofacial gestalt is represented as abnormal facial shape.

edges.description: Cardiac developmental involvement manifests as abnormal heart morphology.

Postnatal fragility includes neonatal respiratory distress.

nodes.description: Generalized muscular hypotonia is a frequent early feature of Ogden syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: The Cardiovascular Manifestations and Management Recommendations for Ogden Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Pathogenic variants within NAA10 cause Ogden Syndrome (OS), which is characterized by varying degrees of intellectual disability, hypotonia, developmental delay, and cardiac abnormalities.

nodes.description: The recurrent NAA10 p.Ser37Pro variant disrupts the catalytic function of the NatA complex and weakens interactions between NAA10 and its binding partners NAA15 and NAA50. Because NatA is the major N-terminal acetyltransferase in human cells, this defect reduces co-translational N-terminal acetylation of selected substrates and establishes the primary molecular lesion in Ogden syndrome. This interpretation is variant-specific: the severe p.Ser37Pro phenotype reflects selective substrate hypoacetylation and NatA complex-assembly defects rather than a nonspecific collapse of all NAA10/NAA15-related acetylation biology.
nodes.id: Impaired NatA catalytic activity and complex assembly

evidence.reference_title: Biochemical and cellular analysis of Ogden syndrome reveals downstream Nt-acetylation defects.
evidence.snippet: Biochemical data further demonstrate a reduced catalytic capacity and an impaired interaction between hNaa10 S37P and Naa15 as well as Naa50 (NatE), another interactor of the NatA complex.

evidence.reference_title: A Saccharomyces cerevisiae model reveals in vivo functional impairment of the Ogden syndrome N-terminal acetyltransferase NAA10 Ser37Pro mutant.
evidence.snippet: Combined, these data provide strong support for the functional impairment of hNaa10 S37P in vivo and suggest that reduced Nt-acetylation of one or more target substrates contributes to the pathogenesis of the Ogden syndrome.

nodes.description: Intellectual disability is a common neurodevelopmental manifestation across the broader Ogden syndrome cohort.

evidence.reference_title: Longitudinal adaptive behavioral outcomes in Ogden syndrome by seizure status and therapeutic intervention.
evidence.snippet: The condition was initially described in 2011 and is characterized by a range of neurologic symptoms, including intellectual disability and seizures, as well as developmental delays, psychiatric symptoms, congenital heart abnormalities, hypotonia, and others.

evidence.reference_title: Confirmation of Ogden syndrome as an X-linked recessive fatal disorder due to a recurrent NAA10 variant and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Ogden syndrome is a rare lethal X-linked recessive disorder caused by a recurrent missense variant (Ser37Pro) in the NAA10 gene

nodes.description: Reduced NatA function in Ogden syndrome causes incomplete N-terminal acetylation of a subset of NatA and NatE substrates rather than a global proteome-wide collapse, defining a selective proteostasis defect downstream of impaired NatA complex activity.
